title: lichen_lora_l2_deinit() clears initialized without stopping RX thread first in abort case
description:
Location: lora_l2.c:388-429

**What goes wrong:** lichen_lora_l2_deinit() checks running state (line 394) but if called after a forced abort, the running flag is already 0 while the thread struct may still be referenced by the kernel. The code comments acknowledge the mutex might be locked by a dead thread, but the fix (reinitializing mutex at line 425) only works if the thread was truly aborted. If k_thread_abort() failed or the thread is in a weird state, k_mutex_init() on a kernel-owned mutex is undefined behavior.

**Trigger:** Hardware fault during RX thread operation where abort doesn't cleanly terminate the thread.

**Fix:** Document this is best-effort recovery. Consider adding a k_thread_join() with short timeout before mutex reinit to ensure thread is actually terminated.
